symptoms.symptom_name: New localised hard, cord-like, warm, erythematous, exquisitely tender segment along a superficial varicosity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hard, tender, warm, red cord following the line of a superficial varicose vein is the classic appearance of superficial thrombophlebitis.
A localised palpable superficial cord is a surface-vein process rather than a deep diffuse thrombosis, though DVT still needs exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long-standing bilateral varicose veins with chronic aching/heaviness worse on standing and relieved by elevation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Varicose veins with stasis in dilated superficial veins predispose to superficial thrombophlebitis.
An elevation-relieved venous pattern of chronic ache argues against an arterial cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No massive whole-calf swelling and no deep generalised calf ache (localised surface process, argues against DVT)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of whole-calf swelling and a deep diffuse ache makes DVT less likely, though it cannot be fully excluded without imaging.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with redness confined linearly along the vein (argues against cellulitis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A spreading hot plaque with fever characterises cellulitis; linear redness along one vein without fever favours superficial thrombophlebitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, breathlessness or haemoptysis (no current PE)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of respiratory symptoms makes current PE unlikely but does not remove the residual risk requiring safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic ache relieved by leg elevation, not worsened by it (venous, not arterial, pattern)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Arterial pain classically worsens on elevation; relief on elevation indicates a venous rather than arterial process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable firm tender superficial venous cord, no DVT signs, easily palpable pedal pulses on examination; venous eczema and haemosiderin staining at the ankles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable firm tender superficial cord with intact pulses and no DVT signs confirms superficial thrombophlebitis with intact arterial supply.

symptoms.symptom_name: Superficial venous reflux and superficial thrombus without deep-vein involvement on venous duplex ultrasound

investigation_details.investigation_name: Venous duplex ultrasound of the lower limb
investigation_details.expected_result: Superficial venous reflux with a segment of superficial thrombus; deep veins compressible/patent (excluding DVT) and thrombus extent relative to the sapheno-femoral junction defined
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Extent of superficial thrombus and a possible coexisting/occult DVT remain undefined, and the underlying reflux is unmapped for elective treatment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Duplex demonstrating superficial thrombus with patent deep veins confirms superficial thrombophlebitis and excludes DVT.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute superficial thrombophlebitis complicating chronic varicose veins
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A new hard, tender, red cord along a known varicosity, with no whole-calf swelling/deep ache and afebrile with linear redness, indicates superficial thrombophlebitis on varicose veins in a systemically-well patient - needing NSAIDs, prompt in-person assessment with DVT exclusion and duplex ultrasound, with DVT/PE and cellulitis safety-netting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical assessment (palpate the cord, examine for DVT, check pedal pulses)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms superficial thrombophlebitis, screens clinically for DVT and confirms intact arterial supply before management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Venous duplex ultrasound of the lower limb
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Maps venous reflux, defines the extent of superficial thrombus relative to the sapheno-femoral junction, and excludes a coexisting DVT.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: DVT exclusion (clinical assessment +/- compression ultrasound) if any DVT features develop
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Superficial thrombophlebitis can propagate to or coexist with DVT; new whole-calf swelling or deep ache warrants urgent DVT exclusion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Ambulance transfer to ED for a stable, systemically-well localised superficial thrombophlebitis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Over-triage: a stable patient with a localised superficial process without DVT/PE features needs prompt in-person assessment, not emergency ambulance transfer.
